name: Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular from El Granado
name_original: Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular desde El Granado
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around El Granado traverse a diverse landscape in Spain's Huelva province, characterized by mountainous terrain, riverine scenery, and agricultural lands. The region features the Guadiana River, offering picturesque views, and the prominent Chanza Dam. Cyclists can explore the Vía Verde del Guadiana, a former railway line, which provides an accessible pathway through varied historical and natural settings. The area's mix of forests, pastures, and rolling hills creates a dynamic environment for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around El Granado The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular from El Granado, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a historic greenway, offering diverse landscapes and views of the Guadiana River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is From Sanlúcar de Guadiana to San Silvestre de Guzmán – circular, a difficult 26.9 miles (43.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and traverses varied terrain between villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Odeleite Reservoir – View of the Guadiana River loop from Sanlúcar de Guadiana, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past the Odeleite Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around El Granado is defined by rolling hills, the Guadiana River, and historical greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in El Granado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Granado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in El Granado?
A: El Granado offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 25 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse landscapes of the Huelva province.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in El Granado?
A: Cycling in El Granado provides a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll traverse mountainous terrain, enjoy picturesque riverine scenery along the Guadiana River, and pass through agricultural lands, forests, and pastures. Notable features include the prominent Chanza Dam and panoramic views that sometimes extend across to Portugal.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in El Granado?
A: Yes, El Granado has routes suitable for less experienced cyclists and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984074487/via-verde-del-bajo-guadiana-circular-from-el-granado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular from El Granado&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.9-mile (35.3 km) trail that follows a historic greenway, offering diverse landscapes and views of the Guadiana River on an accessible pathway. There are 2 easy routes and 15 moderate routes in total.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, El Granado offers 11 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985012594/from-sanlucar-de-guadiana-to-san-silvestre-de-guzman-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sanlúcar de Guadiana to San Silvestre de Guzmán – circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.9-mile (43.4 km) path featuring significant elevation gain and varied terrain between villages. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10291720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odeleite Reservoir – View of the Guadiana River loop from Sanlúcar de Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 30.5 miles (49.1 km) through riverine landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in El Granado?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in El Granado are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984074487/via-verde-del-bajo-guadiana-circular-from-el-granado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular from El Granado&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985012594/from-sanlucar-de-guadiana-to-san-silvestre-de-guzman-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sanlúcar de Guadiana to San Silvestre de Guzmán – circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: El Granado's routes are rich in history. You can visit the restored 18th-century &lt;strong&gt;Molino de Viento&lt;/strong&gt; (Windmill) and its &lt;strong&gt;Museo Etnográfico y de Aperos de Labranza&lt;/strong&gt;. Other architectural highlights include the neoclassical &lt;strong&gt;Iglesia de Santa Catalina&lt;/strong&gt; and the 15th-century Moorish-Gothic &lt;strong&gt;Ermita de la Santísima Trinidad&lt;/strong&gt;. For ancient history enthusiasts, prehistoric burial chambers (dolmens) can be found in local olive groves. The historic hamlet of &lt;strong&gt;Puerto de la Laja&lt;/strong&gt;, once a crucial mineral port, is also worth exploring.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for?
A: The region is dotted with stunning natural features and viewpoints. Along your ride, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4546209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses of Portugal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7321927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful path along the Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; provides picturesque river views. The Vía Verde del Guadiana features the impressive Puente del Lobo, a long bridge with panoramic vistas. The Chanza Dam is another prominent natural landmark.

Q: Is the Vía Verde del Guadiana suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Guadiana is an excellent option for touring cyclists. This 17km greenway follows a former railway line, offering an accessible and scenic pathway through diverse landscapes. It's a great way to experience the region's natural beauty and mining history, culminating at Puerto de la Laja on the Rio Guadiana. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana loop from Minas La Isabel&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.6-mile (29.9 km) route that incorporates this greenway.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in El Granado?
A: The best time for touring cycling in El Granado is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though still rideable on many days.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in El Granado?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Granado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 11 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Guadiana River, and the historical greenways that offer a unique blend of nature and heritage. The variety of routes also appeals to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any nearby towns or settlements worth visiting by bike?
A: Yes, several routes connect El Granado to charming nearby settlements. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985012594/from-sanlucar-de-guadiana-to-san-silvestre-de-guzman-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sanlúcar de Guadiana to San Silvestre de Guzmán – circular&lt;/a&gt; takes you between these villages. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanlúcar de Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque village on the river, which serves as a starting point for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10291720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odeleite Reservoir – View of the Guadiana River loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in El Granado?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, El Granado is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984074487/via-verde-del-bajo-guadiana-circular-from-el-granado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana – circular from El Granado&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from the village, making it convenient to park and begin your ride.
tips: Windmill restored in 1996 with parts brought from Portugal to maintain its character. It works like those of yesteryear thanks to its reconstruction. Inside you can see various old utensils previously used by millers.

This Via Verde (paths for pedestrian, cyclist and equestrian use in the natural environment resulting from the conditioning of disused infrastructures, mainly old railways), runs 17 km through the Huelva mountains of Western Andévalo.

In the direction of La Isabel -&gt; Puerto de La Laja, approximately the initial 45% (more or less up to the place known as Barranco del Lobo) is poorly preserved and the surface has a lot of loose stone of considerable size. In addition it stings continuously upwards, although with insignificant slopes. The remaining 55% is in very good condition, with dirt tracks and well-packed paddocks, also in continuous decline.

Picturesque and quiet town in the interior of Huelva with typical white houses. Among its places of interest is the Solana windmill, the Church of Santa Catalina, the Monument to the Smuggler and a few kilometers away the well-known Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana.
